summary: Story 1.8 is implementation-ready for private photo attachment and authorized media delivery. It is source-backed, tenant-safe, scoped to the existing memory record route/editor/service flow, and gives @dev enough implementation and testing context without expanding into filters, public sharing, visibility controls, maps, masonry, reporting dashboards, account flows, or album-generation language.

template_compliance.notes: Required story sections are present and populated for pre-implementation: Status, Executor Assignment, Story, Acceptance Criteria, Integration Verification, CodeRabbit Integration, Tasks / Subtasks, Dev Notes, Testing, Change Log, Dev Agent Record, and QA Results. Future-owned Dev Agent Record and QA Results placeholders remain, which is expected before implementation.

executor_assignment.notes: Executor @dev and quality_gate @architect are known, distinct agents. quality_gate_tools is non-empty and aligned to the code/frontend/backend route, service, Livewire, storage, policy, media delivery, and route-access slice.

dependency_sequence.notes: Dependencies on Stories 1.1, 1.2, 1.4a, 1.4b, 1.4c, 1.5, 1.6, and 1.7 are explicit. The Story 1.7 file still contains a textual lifecycle mismatch, but the DevOps handoff reports PR #13 merged, release PASS, tag v1.8.0, and this branch prepared from merged mainline.

file_structure.notes: The story identifies the correct Laravel memory product locations: routes/web.php, app/Http/Controllers/MemoryMediaController.php, app/Models/MemoryRecord.php, app/Policies/MemoryRecordPolicy.php, app/Services/MemoryRecordService.php, app/Livewire/Memory/MemoryRecordEditor.php, detail/timeline/editor Blade views, translations, private media config, .env.example if new config is introduced, and focused PHPUnit test paths.

ui_frontend_completeness.notes: The UI guidance is specific enough for implementation: multiple image upload state, validation error feedback, count limits, protected preview states, mobile usability, detail rendering, timeline count/thumbnail states, and accessible labels that do not expose private filenames.

acceptance_criteria_coverage.notes: Tasks map to all six acceptance criteria and all three integration verification points, including diary and period attachment persistence, parent-record connection, 2 GB workspace cap, 10 MB image limit, 25 photos per record, private storage, authorized delivery, public blog-media separation, cross-tenant denial, and S3-compatible future path.

testing_guidance.notes: The story requires focused PHPUnit/Livewire coverage for diary and period photo attachment, multiple upload validation, unsupported types, image-size limits, count limits, workspace cap, parent-record persistence, authorized media delivery, cross-tenant denial, non-member denial, response leakage checks, timeline count behavior, detail rendering, and existing diary/period regression safety.

security_scope.notes: Private storage, route-tenant media re-scoping, explicit media policy behavior, generic denial, no public URLs, no raw paths, no public filenames, no metadata leakage, EXIF/GPS stripping guidance, and public blog-media separation are explicit in AC/IV, scope boundaries, tasks, Dev Notes, and CodeRabbit focus areas.

coderabbit_integration.notes: CodeRabbit is enabled in core-config.yaml and the story contains story type analysis, specialized agents, required quality gate tasks, self-healing behavior, and focus areas matching security/frontend/API/architecture risk.

code_intelligence.notes: Code intelligence duplicate detection was skipped because the local code-intel availability helper was not used in this session and the validate-next-story task permits silent skip when unavailable.

anti_hallucination.notes: Technical claims were cross-checked against PRD Story 1.8, architecture private-media guidance, UI architecture media route and test guidance, framework coding/source-tree rules, current routes/model/service/policy/Livewire code, the SM-to-PO handoff, the DevOps-to-SM Story 1.8 handoff, and the Story 1.7 QA gate.

dev_readiness.notes: The story is self-contained enough for @dev to implement without inventing requirements. The implementation path is constrained to existing service, Livewire, route, model, policy, controller, translation, detail, timeline, private storage, and test surfaces.

nice_to_have: Reconcile stale Story 1.7 textual lifecycle status separately; do not block Story 1.8 because the DevOps handoff establishes the merged baseline.
During implementation, keep edit-time photo changes minimal unless they naturally fall out of the shared editor implementation and are covered by focused tests.
